message:
fix(db): skip ANALYZE + obsolete FTS-trigger migrations on startup; use checkpoint-truncate over compact after atc followup

Two coupled database-layer fixes that keep startup honest now that
Search V3 has decommissioned the old message-FTS path, and swap the
heavyweight compact-then-vacuum on ATC runtime followup for a
simpler WAL checkpoint truncate.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs
- New `is_analyze_migration(id)` skip predicate enumerating the
  three ANALYZE migrations (`v4_analyze_after_indexes`,
  `v13_analyze_after_poller_indexes`, `v16_analyze_atc_experiences`).
  Their `sqlite_stat1` side effects can cause FrankenSQLite to
  reenter schema refresh during query planning, which in turn can
  double-open the connection during startup when the canonical
  lane also holds a write lock. Both lanes now route around
  ANALYZE and the planner rebuilds sample stats on first query.
- New `is_obsolete_message_fts_trigger_migration(id)` skip
  predicate covering the three `v1_create_trigger_messages_*`
  migrations (ai/ad/au). Search V3 decommissioned `fts_messages`,
  and reintroducing these triggers before later table migrations
  now makes SQLite re-parse trigger bodies that reference the
  missing virtual table. Skipping in both lanes keeps base-mode
  and runtime-canonical startup green.
- Doc comment on the existing "migrations excluded from base mode"
  block extended to explain *why* the two new exclusions exist, so
  a future editor doesn't re-enable one without understanding the
  failure mode.
- mod tests gets follow-up coverage asserting each new predicate
  returns true for the expected ids and false for neighbors.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs
- `run_sqlite_init_once`, ATC runtime canonical followup branch:
  swap the call from `compact_sqlite_path(Path::new(sqlite_path))`
  to `wal_checkpoint_truncate_path(Path::new(sqlite_path))`. Error
  stage name updated from `compact_after_atc_followup` to
  `checkpoint_after_atc_followup` so surfaced errors point at the
  actual call.
- Remove the now-unused `compact_sqlite_path` helper entirely. It
  was the only caller of the PRAGMA_DB_INIT_BASE_SQL-then-VACUUM
  pattern and kept us carrying that Vacuum-during-startup risk
  (which on bigger archives could block minutes). Checkpoint
  truncate achieves the same wal-file-is-empty post-condition the
  followup cared about, without the full rewrite.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/schema_migration.rs
- Trivial single-line trim — a trailing blank line in one test
  body dropped now that it's no longer the separator between two
  migrations that shared setup.
